title: Alexander Henderson (1831–1913)
subtitle: Born in Scotland, Henderson emigrated to Canada in 1855 and became a well-known landscape photographer

sentences.en: Alexander Henderson was born in Scotland in 1831 and was the son of a successful merchant.
sentences.zh: 亚历山大·亨德森于1831年出生在苏格兰，是一位成功商人的儿子。

grammar.note: 主语 Alexander Henderson 带两个并列谓语 was born in Scotland in 1831 与 was the son of a successful merchant，由 and 连接。

sentences.en: His grandfather, also called Alexander, had founded the family business, and later became the first chairman of the National Bank of Scotland.
sentences.zh: 他的祖父也叫亚历山大，曾创立了这个家族企业，后来成为苏格兰国家银行的首任董事长。

grammar.type: 过去完成时 + 并列谓语 + 同位语
grammar.note: also called Alexander 为过去分词短语作 His grandfather 的同位补充；主干 His grandfather... had founded the family business, and later became the first chairman...，两谓语并列，had founded 用过去完成时表更早发生。

sentences.en: The family had extensive landholdings in Scotland.
sentences.zh: 这个家族在苏格兰拥有大量地产。

grammar.note: 简单句，主干 The family had extensive landholdings；in Scotland 为地点状语；extensive 意为“广大的、大量的”。

sentences.en: Besides its residence in Edinburgh, it owned Press Estate, 650 acres of farmland about 35 miles southeast of the city.
sentences.zh: 除了在爱丁堡的宅邸外，它还拥有普雷斯庄园——一片位于该市东南约35英里、面积650英亩的农田。

grammar.note: Besides its residence in Edinburgh 为介词短语作状语；主干 it owned Press Estate；650 acres of farmland about 35 miles southeast of the city 为 Press Estate 的同位语。

sentences.en: The family often stayed at Press Castle, the large mansion on the northern edge of the property, and Alexander spent much of his childhood in the area, playing on the beach near Eyemouth or fishing in the streams nearby.
sentences.zh: 这家人经常住在普雷斯城堡——那座位于庄园北缘的大宅——而亚历山大的童年大多在这一带度过，或在艾茅斯附近的海滩上玩耍，或在附近的溪流里钓鱼。

grammar.type: 同位语 + and 并列 + 现在分词状语
grammar.note: the large mansion on the northern edge of the property 为 Press Castle 的同位语；and 连接第二分句 Alexander spent much of his childhood in the area；playing on the beach... or fishing in the streams nearby 为现在分词短语作伴随状语，两动作由 or 并列。

sentences.en: Even after he went to school at Murcheston Academy on the outskirts of Edinburgh, Henderson returned to Press at weekends.
sentences.zh: 即使在他前往爱丁堡郊外的默切斯顿学校上学之后，亨德森周末仍会回到普雷斯庄园。

grammar.note: Even after he went to school at Murcheston Academy... 为 after 引导、由 even 强化的让步时间状语从句；主句 Henderson returned to Press at weekends；on the outskirts of Edinburgh 为地点定语。

sentences.en: In 1849 he began a three-year apprenticeship to become an accountant.
sentences.zh: 1849年，他开始了为期三年的学徒生涯，以成为一名会计师。

grammar.note: 主干 he began a three-year apprenticeship；to become an accountant 为不定式作目的状语；three-year 为复合形容词作定语。

sentences.en: Although he never liked the prospect of a business career, he stayed with it to please his family.
sentences.zh: 尽管他从不喜欢从商这一前景，但为了让家人高兴，他还是坚持了下来。

grammar.type: 让步状语从句 + 不定式目的
grammar.note: Although he never liked the prospect of a business career 为让步状语从句；主句 he stayed with it；to please his family 为不定式作目的状语。

sentences.en: In October 1855, however, he emigrated to Canada with his wife Agnes Elder Robertson and they settled in Montreal.
sentences.zh: 然而，1855年10月，他与妻子阿格尼丝·埃尔德·罗伯逊移居加拿大，二人在蒙特利尔定居下来。

grammar.note: however 为插入转折；两分句由 and 连接：he emigrated to Canada with his wife... 与 they settled in Montreal。

sentences.en: Henderson learned photography in Montreal around the year 1857 and quickly took it up as a serious amateur.
sentences.zh: 亨德森大约在1857年于蒙特利尔学会了摄影，并很快作为一名认真的业余爱好者投身其中。

grammar.type: 并列谓语 + take up 短语
grammar.note: 主语 Henderson 带两并列谓语 learned photography... 与 quickly took it up as a serious amateur；take up 意为“开始从事、培养（爱好）”；as a serious amateur 为方式状语。

sentences.en: He became a personal friend and colleague of the Scottish-Canadian photographer William Notman.
sentences.zh: 他成了苏格兰裔加拿大摄影师威廉·诺特曼的私人朋友和同事。

grammar.note: 主干 He became a personal friend and colleague of...；the Scottish-Canadian photographer 为 William Notman 的前置同位/定语。

sentences.en: The two men made a photographic excursion to Niagara Falls in 1860 and they cooperated on experiments with magnesium flares as a source of artificial light in 1865.
sentences.zh: 两人于1860年前往尼亚加拉瀑布进行摄影远足，又于1865年合作进行以镁光焰作为人造光源的实验。

grammar.note: 两分句由 and 连接：The two men made a photographic excursion to Niagara Falls 与 they cooperated on experiments with magnesium flares；as a source of artificial light 为 magnesium flares 的补充说明。

sentences.en: They belonged to the same societies and were among the founding members of the Art Association of Montreal.
sentences.zh: 他们隶属于相同的社团，并都是蒙特利尔艺术协会的创始成员之一。

grammar.note: 主语 They 带两并列谓语 belonged to the same societies 与 were among the founding members of...；among 表“属于……之列”。

sentences.en: Henderson acted as chairman of the association’s first meeting, which was held in Notman’s studio on 11 January 1860.
sentences.zh: 亨德森担任了该协会首次会议的主席，这次会议于1860年1月11日在诺特曼的工作室举行。

grammar.note: 主干 Henderson acted as chairman of the association's first meeting；which was held in Notman's studio on 11 January 1860 为非限制性定语从句修饰 first meeting；act as 意为“担任、充当”。

sentences.en: In spite of their friendship, their styles of photography were quite different.
sentences.zh: 尽管两人交情深厚，他们的摄影风格却截然不同。

grammar.type: In spite of 让步状语
grammar.note: In spite of their friendship 为介词短语作让步状语，意为“尽管”；主干 their styles of photography were quite different。

sentences.en: While Notman’s landscapes were noted for their bold realism, Henderson for the first 20 years of his career produced romantic images, showing the strong influence of the British landscape tradition.
sentences.zh: 诺特曼的风景作品以其大胆的写实主义著称，而亨德森在其职业生涯的头20年里创作的是浪漫主义风格的作品，显示出英国风景传统的深刻影响。

grammar.type: While 对比状语从句 + 现在分词状语
grammar.note: While Notman's landscapes were noted for their bold realism 为 while 引导的对比状语从句；主句 Henderson... produced romantic images；showing the strong influence of... 为现在分词短语作伴随/结果状语。

sentences.en: His artistic and technical progress was rapid and in 1865 he published his first major collection of landscape photographs.
sentences.zh: 他在艺术和技术上进步迅速，并于1865年出版了他的第一部重要的风景摄影作品集。

grammar.note: 两分句由 and 连接：His artistic and technical progress was rapid 与 he published his first major collection of landscape photographs。

sentences.en: The publication had limited circulation (only seven copies have ever been found), and was called Canadian Views and Studies.
sentences.zh: 这部出版物发行量有限（至今仅发现七本），书名为《加拿大风景与习作》。

grammar.type: 并列谓语 + 括号插入
grammar.note: 主语 The publication 带两并列谓语 had limited circulation 与 was called Canadian Views and Studies；括号内 only seven copies have ever been found 为插入的补充说明（现在完成时被动）。

sentences.en: The contents of each copy vary significantly and have proved a useful source for evaluating Henderson’s early work.
sentences.zh: 每一本的内容差异显著，这已被证明是评估亨德森早期作品的有用资料来源。

grammar.type: 并列谓语 + 现在完成时
grammar.note: 主语 The contents of each copy 带两并列谓语 vary significantly 与 have proved a useful source...；for evaluating Henderson's early work 为介词短语作 source 的后置定语。

sentences.en: In 1866, he gave up his business to open a photographic studio, advertising himself as a portrait and landscape photographer.
sentences.zh: 1866年，他放弃了自己的生意，开了一家摄影工作室，将自己宣传为肖像和风景摄影师。

grammar.type: 不定式目的 + 现在分词状语
grammar.note: 主干 he gave up his business；to open a photographic studio 为不定式作目的状语；advertising himself as a portrait and landscape photographer 为现在分词短语作伴随状语。

sentences.en: From about 1870 he dropped portraiture to specialize in landscape photography and other views.
sentences.zh: 大约从1870年起，他放弃了肖像摄影，专门从事风景摄影和其他景物拍摄。

grammar.note: 主干 he dropped portraiture；to specialize in landscape photography and other views 为不定式作目的状语，specialize in 意为“专门从事”。

sentences.en: His numerous photographs of city life revealed in street scenes, houses, and markets are alive with human activity, and although his favourite subject was landscape he usually composed his scenes around such human pursuits as farming the land, cutting ice on a river, or sailing down a woodland stream.
sentences.zh: 他大量表现城市生活的照片——街景、房屋和市场——充满了人的活动气息；尽管他最喜爱的主题是风景，他通常仍围绕诸如耕地、在河上凿冰或顺着林间溪流泛舟这类人类活动来构图。

grammar.type: 过去分词定语 + and + 让步从句
grammar.note: 前一分句主语 His numerous photographs of city life，revealed in street scenes, houses, and markets 为过去分词定语，谓语 are alive with human activity；and 后 although his favourite subject was landscape 为让步从句，主句 he usually composed his scenes around such human pursuits as...，三个动名词并列。

sentences.en: There was sufficient demand for these types of scenes and others he took depicting the lumber trade, steamboats and waterfalls to enable him to make a living.
sentences.zh: 对这些类型的景物，以及他所拍摄的表现木材贸易、蒸汽船和瀑布的其他作品，都有足够的需求，足以使他维持生计。

grammar.type: there be + 省略关系词定语从句 + 不定式结果
grammar.note: 主干 There was sufficient demand for these types of scenes and others；(that) he took depicting the lumber trade... 为省略关系词定语从句修饰 others，depicting... 为现在分词定语；to enable him to make a living 为不定式作结果状语。

sentences.en: There was little competing hobby or amateur photography before the late 1880s because of the time-consuming techniques involved and the weight of the equipment.
sentences.zh: 在19世纪80年代末之前，几乎不存在与之竞争的业余爱好或业余摄影，这是由于所涉及的技术耗时费力以及设备笨重。

grammar.type: there be + because of 原因状语
grammar.note: 主干 There was little competing hobby or amateur photography；because of the time-consuming techniques involved and the weight of the equipment 为 because of 引导的原因状语，involved 为过去分词定语修饰 techniques。

sentences.en: People wanted to buy photographs as souvenirs of a trip or as gifts, and catering to this market, Henderson had stock photographs on display at his studio for mounting, framing, or inclusion in albums.
sentences.zh: 人们想购买照片作为旅行的纪念品或作为礼物，为迎合这一市场，亨德森在工作室里陈列了备用照片，供裱贴、装框或收入相册之用。

grammar.type: 并列句 + 现在分词状语
grammar.note: 前一分句 People wanted to buy photographs as souvenirs... or as gifts；and 后 catering to this market 为现在分词状语，主句 Henderson had stock photographs on display...；for mounting, framing, or inclusion in albums 为目的状语，三名词并列。

sentences.en: Henderson frequently exhibited his photographs in Montreal and abroad, in London, Edinburgh, Dublin, Paris, New York, and Philadelphia.
sentences.zh: 亨德森经常在蒙特利尔以及海外——伦敦、爱丁堡、都柏林、巴黎、纽约和费城——展出他的照片。

grammar.type: 主谓宾 + 并列地点状语
grammar.note: 主干 Henderson frequently exhibited his photographs；in Montreal and abroad 与其后一系列城市名为并列/同位地点状语。

sentences.en: He met with greater success in 1877 and 1878 in New York when he won first prizes in the exhibition held by E and H T Anthony and Company for landscapes using the Lambertype process.
sentences.zh: 1877年和1878年他在纽约取得了更大的成功，当时他凭借采用兰伯特工艺制作的风景作品，在E and H T Anthony公司举办的展览中赢得了一等奖。

grammar.type: when 时间状语从句 + 过去分词定语
grammar.note: 主句 He met with greater success in 1877 and 1878 in New York；when he won first prizes in the exhibition 为时间状语从句；held by... 为过去分词定语修饰 exhibition，using the Lambertype process 为现在分词定语修饰 landscapes；meet with 意为“获得、遭遇”。

sentences.en: In 1878 his work won second prize at the world exhibition in Paris.
sentences.zh: 1878年，他的作品在巴黎世界博览会上荣获二等奖。

grammar.note: 简单句，主干 his work won second prize；at the world exhibition in Paris 为地点状语。

sentences.en: In the 1870s and 1880s Henderson travelled widely throughout Quebec and Ontario, in Canada, documenting the major cities of the two provinces and many of the villages in Quebec.
sentences.zh: 在19世纪70和80年代，亨德森走遍了加拿大的魁北克和安大略两省，记录下两省的主要城市以及魁北克的许多村庄。

grammar.note: 主干 Henderson travelled widely throughout Quebec and Ontario；documenting the major cities... and many of the villages 为现在分词短语作伴随状语。

sentences.en: He was especially fond of the wilderness and often travelled by canoe on the Blanche, du Lièvre, and other noted eastern rivers.
sentences.zh: 他尤其钟爱荒野，常常划着独木舟在布朗什河、迪列夫尔河等著名的东部河流上航行。

grammar.type: 并列谓语 + be fond of
grammar.note: 主语 He 带两并列谓语 was especially fond of the wilderness 与 often travelled by canoe on...；be fond of 意为“喜爱”；by canoe 为方式状语。

sentences.en: He went on several occasions to the Maritimes and in 1872 he sailed by yacht along the lower north shore of the St Lawrence River.
sentences.zh: 他数次前往海洋省份，并于1872年乘游艇沿圣劳伦斯河下游的北岸航行。

grammar.note: 两分句由 and 连接：He went on several occasions to the Maritimes 与 he sailed by yacht along...；on several occasions 意为“多次”。

sentences.en: That same year, while in the lower St Lawrence River region, he took some photographs of the construction of the Intercolonial Railway.
sentences.zh: 同年，他在圣劳伦斯河下游地区时，拍摄了一些殖民地间铁路施工的照片。

grammar.note: 主句 he took some photographs of the construction of the Intercolonial Railway；while in the lower St Lawrence River region 为省略主语和 be 的时间状语从句（=while he was in...）；That same year 为时间状语。

sentences.en: This undertaking led in 1875 to a commission from the railway to record the principal structures along the almost-completed line connecting Montreal to Halifax.
sentences.zh: 这项工作使他在1875年获得铁路方面的委托，去记录连接蒙特利尔与哈利法克斯、即将完工的那条铁路沿线的主要建筑。

grammar.type: lead to + 不定式定语 + 现在分词定语
grammar.note: 主干 This undertaking led in 1875 to a commission from the railway；to record the principal structures 为不定式作 commission 的后置定语；connecting Montreal to Halifax 为现在分词定语修饰 line。

sentences.en: Commissions from other railways followed. In 1876 he photographed bridges on the Quebec, Montreal, Ottawa and Occidental Railway between Montreal and Ottawa.
sentences.zh: 来自其他铁路公司的委托接踵而至。1876年，他拍摄了蒙特利尔与渥太华之间“魁北克-蒙特利尔-渥太华-西部铁路”上的桥梁。

grammar.note: 首句 Commissions from other railways followed，followed 为不及物动词“接着发生”；后句 he photographed bridges on the... Railway，between Montreal and Ottawa 为地点状语。

sentences.en: In 1885 he went west along the Canadian Pacific Railway (CPR) as far as Rogers Pass in British Columbia, where he took photographs of the mountains and the progress of construction.
sentences.zh: 1885年，他沿加拿大太平洋铁路（CPR）西行，一直到不列颠哥伦比亚省的罗杰斯山口，在那里拍摄了群山和施工进展的照片。

grammar.note: 主句 he went west along the Canadian Pacific Railway as far as Rogers Pass；as far as 意为“远至”；where he took photographs of... 为定语从句修饰 Rogers Pass。

sentences.en: In 1892 Henderson accepted a full-time position with the CPR as manager of a photographic department which he was to set up and administer.
sentences.zh: 1892年，亨德森接受了加拿大太平洋铁路的一个全职职位，担任一个摄影部门的经理，而这个部门要由他来筹建和管理。

grammar.type: 定语从句 + be to do
grammar.note: 主干 Henderson accepted a full-time position with the CPR as manager of a photographic department；which he was to set up and administer 为定语从句修饰 department，was to do 表示“将要、注定要”。

sentences.en: His duties included spending four months in the field each year.
sentences.zh: 他的职责包括每年花四个月时间在野外工作。

grammar.note: 主干 His duties included spending four months in the field each year，include 后接动名词短语作宾语；in the field 意为“在野外、在实地”。

sentences.en: That summer he made his second trip west, photographing extensively along the railway line as far as Victoria.
sentences.zh: 那年夏天，他第二次西行，沿铁路线一直到维多利亚进行了大量拍摄。

grammar.note: 主干 he made his second trip west；photographing extensively along the railway line as far as Victoria 为现在分词短语作伴随状语。

sentences.en: He continued in this post until 1897, when he retired completely from photography.
sentences.zh: 他一直担任这一职务直到1897年，那时他彻底退出了摄影行业。

grammar.note: 主句 He continued in this post until 1897；when he retired completely from photography 为 when 引导的非限制性定语从句修饰 1897；retire from 意为“从……退休/退出”。

sentences.en: When Henderson died in 1913, his huge collection of glass negatives was stored in the basement of his house.
sentences.zh: 亨德森于1913年去世时，他数量庞大的玻璃底片收藏被存放在他家的地下室里。

grammar.type: 时间状语从句 + 被动语态
grammar.note: When Henderson died in 1913 为时间状语从句；主句 his huge collection of glass negatives was stored in the basement（被动）。

sentences.en: Today collections of his work are held at the National Archives of Canada, Ottawa, and the McCord Museum of Canadian History, Montreal.
sentences.zh: 如今，他的作品收藏分别存放于渥太华的加拿大国家档案馆和蒙特利尔的麦考德加拿大历史博物馆。

grammar.type: 被动语态 + 并列地点状语
grammar.note: 主干 collections of his work are held at...（被动）；the National Archives of Canada, Ottawa 与 the McCord Museum of Canadian History, Montreal 为并列地点状语。

questions.title: Questions 1–8
instructions: Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
In boxes 1–8 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

items.prompt: Henderson rarely visited the area around Press estate when he was younger.
Henderson pursued a business career because it was what his family wanted.
Henderson and Notman were surprised by the results of their 1865 experiment.
There were many similarities between Henderson’s early landscapes and those of Notman.
The studio that Henderson opened in 1866 was close to his home.
Henderson gave up portraiture so that he could focus on taking photographs of scenery.
When Henderson began work for the Intercolonial Railway, the Montreal to Halifax line had been finished.
Henderson’s last work as a photographer was with the Canadian Pacific Railway.

questions.title: Questions 9–13
instructions: Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 9–13 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt: Early life: was born in Scotland in 1831 – father was a 9 ____
Start of a photographic career: people bought Henderson’s photos because photography took up considerable time and the 10 ____ was heavy
the photographs Henderson sold were 11 ____ or souvenirs
Travelling as a professional photographer: took many trips along eastern rivers in a 12 ____
worked for CPR in 1885 and photographed the 13 ____ and the railway at Rogers Pass
